chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1621 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For katcha peshi. 25.11.2016. Mr. Irshad Ahmed Channa, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ameer Ali Brohi, Advocate alongwith respondent No.4. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional A.G a/w ASI Shahzad Khan of P.S Airport Nawabshah. = Today, the comments have been filed on behalf of official respondent through the learned A.A.G Sindh, in which the allegations of harassment are categorically denied by the official respondents. The SHO P.S Airport Nawabshah-Shahzad Khan is also present in person and has undertaken that police officials are not involved in causing any harassment to the petitioner and there might be some civil dispute between the petitioner and respondent No.4. Respondent No.4 is also present in person alongwith his Counsel Mr. Ameer Ali Brohi, who files his Vakalatnama as well as written reply, wherein, inter-alia, it is submitted that no harassment was ever caused by the said respondent No.4 to the petitioner. However, there was some business dispute between the petitioner and respondent and this allegation has been refuted by the petitioner side, who submits that that there was some business dealing between the petitioner’s brother and respondent No.4. Be that as it may, the respondents are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner in any manner whatsoever and if the respondent No.4 has some genuine grievances against the petitioner, the same can be redressed through due process of law and not otherwise. With these observations and directions, the present petition stands disposed of.

Be that as it may, the respondents are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner in any manner whatsoever and if the respondent No.4 has some genuine grievances against the petitioner, the same can be redressed through due process of law and not otherwise. With these observations and directions, the present petition stands disposed of. JUDGE Shahid
